title: Bank of China Tower
description: The Bank of China Tower in Hong Kong is one of Asia's most iconic skyscrapers, designed by architect I.M. Pei and completed in 1989. Standing at 367 meters (1,205 feet), it served as Hong Kong's tallest building upon completion and houses the headquarters of Bank of China (Hong Kong) in the city's Central district. Its striking geometric, faceted exterior of triangular glass and aluminum panels was inspired by bamboo stalks, symbolizing growth and prosperity — though feng shui practitioners criticized its sharp angles for supposedly directing negative energy toward neighboring buildings. The tower's innovative composite space frame structure also allowed it to be built using significantly less steel than a conventional skyscraper of its size.

title: San Francisco Main Library
description: San Francisco Main Library, located in the Civic Center district, was designed by Pei Cobb Freed & Partners and opened in 1996 as a modern complement to the Beaux-Arts City Hall across the street. The six-story, 376,000-square-foot building features a distinctive curved glass atrium that floods the interior with natural light and serves as its organizational core. Beyond its vast collection of books and digital resources, the library is notable for its dedicated community centers serving the deaf, blind, and LGBT communities — one of the first public libraries in the U.S. to do so. The building sparked a famous debate after opening over whether its design prioritized open space at the expense of shelf capacity, making it a flashpoint in the broader conversation about form versus function in civic architecture.

title: Seoul International Airport
description: Incheon International Airport, located on Yeongjong Island about 70 km west of Seoul, is South Korea's primary international gateway and one of the most acclaimed airports in the world. Opened in 2001 to replace the aging Gimpo Airport, it has grown into a massive hub handling over 70 million passengers annually. Designed by a consortium led by Fentress Architects, the terminal features a sweeping, light-filled aesthetic inspired by traditional Korean architecture. The airport holds a 5-star Skytrax rating and is consistently ranked among the best globally for its cleanliness, efficiency, and passenger experience. Beyond its operational excellence, Incheon is famous for its extraordinary amenities — including an indoor ice skating rink, cultural museums, a transit hotel, and a golf course — making it as much a destination as a departure point.

title: Paisano Green Community
description: Paisano Green Community in El Paso, Texas is the first Net Zero, fossil fuel-free, LEED Platinum affordable senior housing project in the United States. Developed in 2012 by the Housing Authority of the City of El Paso (HACEP), the 73-unit community for low-income seniors also includes a community center, rooftop terrace, and garden trails. Designed by Boulder-based WORKSHOP8 through a national design competition, the project combines passive design strategies with active systems — including a rooftop 182-kilowatt photovoltaic array and wind turbines — to generate nearly all of its energy on-site. Though the per-unit cost of roughly $203,000 was nearly twice that of a typical El Paso apartment, a life-cycle analysis showed it would cost 20% less over 50 years when factoring in utility and maintenance savings.

title: Aspen Wing House
description: The N'Vitale Residence by CCY Architects is a striking contemporary home on a six-acre site outside Aspen where evergreen forest, Gambel oak, and aspen grove converge. The design splits into two masses — a main living volume and guest wing — built from weathered Corten steel and board-formed concrete, linked by a glass corridor. Global Design News Its signature winged roof lifts from the steel enclosure to create south-facing clerestory strips that capture sunlight on an otherwise north-facing site. Global Design News Designed for art collectors, the home integrates gallery-quality exhibition spaces throughout and earned a 2024 American Architecture Award from The Chicago Athenaeum.

title: Odonata Boulder
description: Odonata is a boutique collection of ten luxury townhomes at 260 Pearl Street in Boulder, Colorado, perched at the mouth of Boulder Canyon where the city meets the foothills. Each of the ten residences offers approximately 3,600 square feet of finished living space with floor-to-ceiling windows framing dramatic Flatiron views, expansive outdoor decks on every level, and a private in-unit elevator. Shared amenities include a gated entry, pool, and sundeck. Finishes throughout — hardwood floors, Spanish tile, Italian cabinetry — set a high benchmark, making Odonata one of Boulder's most refined small-scale residential developments, with urban walkability and immediate mountain access in equal measure.

title: Queens Center
description: Queens Center in Elmhurst, Queens opened in 1973, developed by Taubman Centers and designed by **Victor Gruen Associates** — the firm credited with inventing the modern American shopping mall. Built on the site of the former Fairyland amusement park, it expanded significantly in 2002–2004, reaching nearly **967,000 square feet**. The only super-regional mall in Queens, it occupies one of NYC's busiest intersections with direct subway access. Its sales-per-square-foot have ranked among the highest in the U.S. — nearly triple the national average at their peak.

title: 4th Street House
description: 4th Street House is a contemporary luxury residence at 3307 4th Street in Boulder's Newlands neighborhood. Its striking exterior combines horizontal cedar cladding with board-formed concrete and a black steel frame — a material palette that is both warm and rigorous. Inside, soaring ceilings, floor-to-ceiling windows, and seamless indoor-outdoor flow through LaCantina sliding doors create an exceptionally light-filled living experience. Set on a 9,600-square-foot lot, the four-bedroom home represents some of the finest small-scale residential design in Boulder.

title: Del Amo Fashion Center
description: Del Amo Fashion Center in Torrance, California is a three-level regional shopping mall managed by Simon Property Group, spanning over 2.5 million square feet — the seventh largest mall in the United States. Originally formed in 1981 through the merger of two neighboring malls, it briefly held the title of largest enclosed shopping center in the country until the Mall of America opened in 1992. A major redesign by 5+design transformed the complex with light, glass, and open architecture, creating a human-centered retail experience complete with garden dining terraces, living green walls, skylights, and a 'city within a city' atmosphere of distinct shopping neighborhoods.

title: The Mark
description: The Mark on Pearl is a boutique urban infill development at 2304 Pearl Street in Boulder, Colorado, just east of the iconic Pearl Street Mall. Developed by Pace Development and completed in 2018–2019, the project transformed a formerly commercial property into eight luxury residences featuring loft-like interiors, large-scale windows, private decks, and rooftop terraces with commanding Flatiron views. Units range from 1,500 to nearly 2,000 square feet across standalone homes and townhomes, each with a private garage. The development strikes a balance rare in Boulder — high-end finishes and modern design at a human scale, steps from world-class dining, shopping, and the natural beauty of the Front Range.

title: Valdez Perry Library
description: Valdez-Perry Branch Library is a small but beloved 4,000-square-foot branch of the Denver Public Library, serving the historically isolated Globeville, Elyria, and Swansea neighborhoods of north-central Denver. Drawing inspiration from the classic Carnegie Library, the design centers on a light-filled reading room with restrained materials — concrete masonry, maple ceilings, and red slate floors. Named after civic leaders Bernard Valdez and John Perry, it opened in 1996 and ranks among the highest-circulating branches in the Denver system, a testament to the power of thoughtful civic architecture in underserved communities.

title: US Holocaust Memorial Museum
description: The United States Holocaust Memorial Museum opened in Washington, D.C. in 1993. This 285,000-square-foot structure wears a neoclassical exterior that fits quietly into D.C.'s monumental streetscape. Inside, the architecture shifts dramatically — abstract forms drawn from  visits to Holocaust sites across Europe are deliberately designed to evoke deception, fear, and disorientation. Industrial details reference the machinery of systematic atrocity, while the open-ended spatial form invites each visitor to experience the building through their own lens of memory.

title: Misato Shopping Center
description: LaLaport Shin-Misato is a popular open-plan shopping mall located in Misato, Saitama Prefecture, just north of Tokyo and a short walk from Shin-Misato Station on the JR Musashino Line. Opened in September 2009, it is part of the Mitsui Shopping Park LaLaport chain — one of Japan's most recognized retail brands. Its open-spaced design gives the mall a relaxed, almost coastal ambience that makes it feel larger than its two floors suggest, setting it apart from the more overwhelming mega-malls common to the Tokyo metro area. Home to around 180 stores spanning fashion, interior design, entertainment, and food, the mall houses a strong mix of Japanese and international retailers.
